subject: Sociology
title: The Invention of Whiteness
description: A quick win: understand The Invention of Whiteness.

hook.text: Irish, Italians, and Jews weren't always considered white in America. Whiteness is a category that keeps expanding.

buildup.text: In colonial America, whiteness was created to divide European and African laborers who once worked side by side.

discovery.text: Bacon's Rebellion in 1676 united poor whites and Blacks. Elites responded by giving whites legal privileges.

twist.text: Groups became white when it served economic or political purposes. The boundary was always strategic, never natural.

climax.text: Whiteness isn't an identity with content. It's a position of privilege that defines itself by exclusion.

punchline.text: Whiteness wasn't discovered. It was invented, and recently.

quiz.question: Why was the category of whiteness created in colonial America?
options: To divide laborers along racial lines after Bacon's Rebellion
To describe a natural biological grouping
